explainability.findings: A company-owned blog post outlines HHS guidelines that promote explainability.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Publish user-facing explanation interfaces or documented appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned blog post, "AI Bias in Health Care," provides evidence for the pillars of **explainability, fairness, governance, and transparency**. The post outlines HHS guidelines that promote equity, transparency, and explainability, and discusses bias reduction and vetting data/monitoring models, supporting fairness and governance. It also addresses algorithmic discrimination protections and data governance, reflecting policy principles for AI risk management and transparency.
relevant_sources.title: AI Bias in Health Care

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: A company-owned blog post outlines HHS guidelines that promote equity. This post discusses bias reduction, vetting data, and monitoring models, and addresses algorithmic discrimination protections.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned blog post, "AI Bias in Health Care," provides evidence for the pillars of **explainability, fairness, governance, and transparency**. governance.findings: A company-owned blog post discusses vetting data and monitoring models, supporting governance. The post also addresses data governance.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned blog post, "AI Bias in Health Care," provides evidence for the pillars of **explainability, fairness, governance, and transparency**. oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: A company-owned blog post outlines HHS guidelines that promote transparency. This post also addresses policy principles for AI risk management and transparency.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned blog post, "AI Bias in Health Care," provides evidence for the pillars of **explainability, fairness, governance, and transparency**. The post outlines HHS guidelines that promote equity, transparency, and explainability, and discusses bias reduction and vetting data/monitoring models, supporting fairness and governance. summary.overall_findings: Solventum's published materials include discussions on vetting data and monitoring models, supporting governance practices. These disclosures also address policy principles for AI risk management and transparency, alongside discussions on bias reduction for fairness, indicating documented evidence for 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Explainability is also addressed through published materials outlining HHS guidelines. The assessment draws on a review of 8 publicly available sources, but no qualifying public evidence was found for oversight, privacy, and 1 additional pillar.
